TextENGNew: ``...I think... that's everything.``
Rena's confession came to its close.
No one applauded. No one called for an encore.
...A quiet, solitary stage... where Rena performed alone.
I realized... I'd forgotten to blink for a very long time.
The same seemed true for the others.
Rena surveyed us all with an expression that seemed to ask,@k "Any questions?"
We recognized that she was imitating what Chie-sensei does at the end of a lesson, and everyone managed a small, pained smile in response.

``...Just before coming here, I'd been out by Yagouchi.@k@vS09/02/170200431. Looking for a place to bury the bags.@k@vS09/02/170200432. I wanted somewhere they'd never be found, and ahaha, it was harder than I thought.``

``I even considered burying them in my own backyard—it might actually be safer.@k@vS09/02/170200434. ...But that'd be risky, you know?@k@vS09/02/170200435. Dad might suddenly decide he wants to start a vegetable garden.``
``The deep mountains are better after all.@k@vS09/02/170200437. I didn't want that woman anywhere near Dad, even in death.``
I was overwhelmed with guilt...
...Rena's plan had been going perfectly.
If only I hadn't insisted on changing the game, if I hadn't pushed my stupid idea...
changeReason: Old translation references specific mahjong tiles; new is more general about 'changing the game.' The Japanese 悪ふざけして、変な無理強い ('fooling around and forcing a strange change') aligns better with the new, broader phrasing.

TextENGNew: Looking around, the others all seemed gripped by the same remorse.
If Mion hadn't suggested playing at the junk heaps.
If Satoko hadn't found Rena's hiding place.
changeReason: Old says Satoko found 'broken refrigerator' but Japanese says she found Rena at the garbage heap.

TextENGNew: If Rika-chan... had somehow stopped us from going.
Then Rena would have spent just a few more days and finished everything—in a way we would never have known about.
And after that?
...Nothing would have changed.
Everything would have stayed the same—before and after, always the same.@k Just peaceful, happy days continuing on.
Rena had endured those painful days alone, keeping up the pretense of normalcy for our sake.
And eventually, that pretense would have become real normalcy.
...None of us would ever have noticed what Rena had been through.
In time, even Rena would have... forgotten the bitter memory of taking lives, living as if nothing had happened.
All those small, accumulated efforts—those courageous efforts—every last one!@k ...reduced to nothing.
``I know killing people is wrong.@k@vS09/02/170200439. ...I didn't want to do it, and I never want to again.``
``But... I believe this was the best I could do, and I don't feel the slightest bit of guilt.``

``I simply did everything in my power within the equal chance God gave me to reclaim my happy, peaceful life!@k@vS09/02/170200442. Saying people should accept poverty and suffering with grace is nothing but pretty words from those who already have everything!``

``No one in the world has the right to belittle that effort!@k@vS09/02/170200444. They should be praising me!@k@vS09/02/170200445. They should be celebrating me!@k@vS09/02/170200446. Rena Ryuugu fought against her own fate.@k@vS09/02/170200447. And she won!!``
...At that moment, for some reason, I spoke up.
It was probably the wrong thing to say.
But I felt it needed to be said. So I said it.
``But Rena...@k@vS09/01/170100312. Why... why didn't you come to us?``
``...Come to you?``
``That's right...!@k@vS09/01/170100314. We're friends, aren't we?@k@vS09/01/170100315. Aren't friends supposed to be on your side no matter what—like family?!``
``...If you'd come to us, we might have been able to do something.@k@vS09/01/170100317. Then you wouldn't have had to dirty your own hands!``
The truth was... Rena's words had been making me sad this whole time.
That's why I couldn't hold it in.
Rena had declared, right to our faces, that she'd done the best she could.@k That her very best effort—
In other words, that was the same as declaring to us that@k ...consulting her friends@k had never been part of her best effort from the very start.
changeReason: Old translation condenses the meaning to 'she didn't need us to do the best she could'; new preserves the structure showing that consulting friends was never part of her 'best effort' plan from the start, a more nuanced accusation.

TextENGNew: ``...Kei-chan, saying that now won't...``

``No—please, let me say this.@k@vS09/01/170100319. I believe friends exist to share both joy and suffering!@k@vS09/01/170100320. If you'd talked to us, I believe we could have found a better future together!``
``A better future?@k@vS09/02/170200450. There's no such thing.``
``This is the best possible future.``
``THAT'S A LIE!!!``
Rena flinched at my shout.
Yes... Rena didn't really believe this was the best possible future.
It was merely the future she'd been forced to choose after agonizing with no other options.
If she'd had any other choice, she would never have chosen this one.
``The proof is right there—you've been crying this entire time!!``
``What?!@k@vS09/02/170200454. Who's crying?@k@vS09/02/170200455. Me?!@k@vS09/02/170200456. When have I shed a single tear?!``
``Right now!!@k@vS09/01/170100323. You've been crying the whole time!@k@vS09/01/170100324. Can't you even tell?!``
In truth, Rena's cheeks weren't wet at all.
But that didn't mean she wasn't crying.
Rena had been shedding tears this entire time.
Tears that grieved a cruel fate—she'd fought so hard to be happy, only to end up trapped in this agonizing dead end. Tears painful just to look at.
Anyone who couldn't see those tears wasn't truly thinking about Rena...!
``I'm not crying.@k@vS09/02/170200458. Ahahahaha, as if I'd cry.@k@vS09/02/170200459. I did the right thing. I made the best choice.@k@vS09/02/170200460. Why would I possibly need to cry?``
``Ahahahaha, Keiichi you dummy, I have no idea what you're saying, ahahahaha.``
``That right there... that's what I mean when I say you're CRYING!!!``
Rena, who'd been laughing derisively and spinning around as if dancing, stopped dead. Her expression went flat and dry.
``Fine then.@k@vS09/02/170200463. Let's say I did what you suggested.@k@vS09/02/170200464. I told you all about Dad and that woman.@k@vS09/02/170200465. Then what? What would you have done?``
``...Well, first we'd... um...``
...Rena had never spoken to me with such cutting malice before.
...That mocking, accusing gaze left me speechless...
``You don't know what last year was like, Keiichi-kun.``
``...Last year?``
``Yes.@k@vS09/02/170200470. Last year.@k@vS09/02/170200471. ...June of Showa 57.@k@vS09/02/170200472. The story of someone who was trapped in a labyrinth of misfortune with no exit but murder.``
Satoko gasped.
``You don't know much about him, do you, Keiichi-kun?@k@vS09/02/170200472_2. About Satoshi Houjou-kun.``
...I knew he was Satoko's brother.
And that he'd vanished under the guise of a "transfer."
...There were persistent rumors that he'd killed his aunt for Satoko's sake.
...Because of all that, Satoshi was treated as a taboo subject in class—no, in all of Hinamizawa.
``Satoshi-kun was... just like me.@k@vS09/02/170200474. All he wanted was happy, peaceful days.@k@vS09/02/170200475. All he wanted was to live quietly with his sister.``
``...You've heard the rumors about what kind of person Satoko-chan's aunt was, right, Keiichi-kun?``
...If the little I'd heard was true:
After the parents died, the uncle and aunt who took Satoko and her brother in were wretched people.
The aunt, in particular, was relentlessly cruel to Satoko.
From what I'd heard, Satoshi was the kind of guy who couldn't hurt a fly.
...Even someone like that was driven to kill her. That's how vile the aunt had been.
changeReason: Old says aunt was 'the only one he didn't have a problem killing' but Japanese means 'Even he was driven to kill' -- opposite characterization.

TextENGNew: ``Everyone in Hinamizawa knew about the trouble in the Houjou household.@k@vS09/02/170200478. That the aunt was hysterical and picked fights with everyone, that she tormented Satoshi-kun and Satoko-chan for no reason—everyone knew every last detail.``
``Of course, everyone in our class knew too.``
``Mii-chan, as the heir to one of the Three Families, and Rika-chan too—they both knew. And Satoko-chan was the one living through it.``
``The 'friends' you talk about understood Satoshi-kun's suffering better than anyone in Hinamizawa.``
``...And what happened? Did any of them help Satoshi-kun?``

``No. Not one.@k@vS09/02/170200484. Mii-chan had the most power of all of us, but she hid behind vague excuses about village politics, and in the end, all she did was offer irresponsible sympathy that only wounded him further!``
changeReason: Old says Mion 'made up a lame excuse, saying that it might create a conflict between the Sonozaki family and the villagers'; new says she 'hid behind vague excuses about village politics.' The old version specifies the excuse content (Sonozaki conflict) while the new is vaguer, losing specific detail.

TextENGNew: ``Th-That's not true...!!``
``YES IT IS!!!``

``...Satoshi-kun knew.@k@vS09/02/170200487. He knew you were only pretending to sympathize while having no intention of actually helping.@k@vS09/02/170200488. That's why Satoshi-kun came to hate you, Mii-chan.``
``You talked about being friends, but in the end, you were just another Sonozaki—just another person tormenting the Houjou family!!``
``I-I didn't...!``
``You left him to rot!!!@k@vS09/02/170200491. You rejected his plea for help with the word 'sympathy'!!@k@vS09/02/170200492. Do you have any idea how much that hurt him?!``
Mion struggled desperately to find words to say in her defense... but there was nothing she could say.@k ...In the end, she could only swallow her words.
``And Rika-chan—you were the same.``
The blade of Rena's words swung toward Rika-chan.
...Unlike Mion, Rika-chan's face was utterly expressionless—dry, just like Rena's.
``You may not have Mii-chan's kind of influence, Rika-chan.@k@vS09/02/170200495. But you had tremendous sway over the elders who visit the shrine, didn't you?!``
``There was something you could have done—and you didn't!!``

``In the end, Rika-chan, you were the same as Mii-chan.@k@vS09/02/170200498. You just put on a pitying face and played the innocent little girl!!@k@vS09/02/170200499. All you ever did was comfort Satoshi-kun and Satoko-chan!@k@vS09/02/170200500. You never once tried to address the root of their problem!!``
``Can you even imagine how cruelly merciless that pity was?!@k@vS09/02/170200502. What Satoshi-kun wanted was salvation.@k@vS09/02/170200503. He wanted someone to help him!``
``He was trapped in a situation he couldn't escape alone!! Sinking neck-deep in a swamp, stretching his hand out as far as it could reach, hoping desperately that someone would grab it!!``
``...You're right.``
Rika-chan spoke in a mature voice I'd never heard from her before.
``...I didn't know how to save them, so all I did was offer comfort.@k@vS09/05/170500052. I won't deny the sin of growing complacent with that and never continuing to search for a way to save them.``
``...So you do understand...``
changeReason: Old translation ('I didn't know you knew') expresses surprise at Rika's knowledge; new ('So you do understand') is an acknowledgment of Rika's comprehension. The Japanese 分かってるじゃない means 'you do understand, don't you'—the new is more accurate.

TextENGNew: Rena smiled with a twisted expression.@k ...It was a truly ugly, painful expression to look at.
``And Satoko-chan.@k@vS09/02/170200507. I don't know if you'll believe me, but... your sin is the heaviest of all.``
changeReason: Old says 'weighed down with sin, too'; new says 'your sin is the heaviest of all' -- different severity.

TextENGNew: I didn't understand why Rena would turn on Satoko and tried to shield her... but when I saw Satoko's expression, I froze.
Her face... looked as though she was accepting the sin Rena spoke of.
Perhaps she wanted to confess it herself before Rena's words cut through her.
That's why it was Satoko who spoke first.
``...I don't know if you'll believe me, but... I noticed it on my own.@k@vS09/04/170400076. ...I know what my sin is.``
``I realized it on the night Nii-nii disappeared.@k@vS09/04/170400078. ...I just wish I'd realized it while he was still here...``
``...I understand that feeling.@k@vS09/02/170200510. That sense of realizing too late for regrets... I know it well.``
``That's why, when the same thing happened to me, I chose to fight.``
``...Satoko-chan, I'm sure you can atone for that sin someday.@k@vS09/02/170200513. As long as you've recognized it.``
``...But even so... Nii-nii isn't coming back.``
Satoko looked down, anguish written across her face.
...It was the opposite of what she usually said.
Normally, Satoko insisted that Satoshi would definitely come back someday.
...And yet now... she accepted, without resistance, that he wasn't coming back.
...As if being blood-related meant she simply... knew.
She turned to me suddenly.
``This is what your 'friends' looked like in 1982.@k@vS09/02/170200516. Satoshi-kun told everyone he thought might help him.``

``He said he needed help. He said he was suffering.@k@vS09/02/170200518. He opened up, he consulted them.@k@vS09/02/170200519. He even came to me, and I'd only just transferred in.@k@vS09/02/170200520. There was nothing shameful about that.``

``...Truly unhappy people are desperate!@k@vS09/02/170200522. Satoshi-kun was doing everything he could on his own.@k@vS09/02/170200523. When that wasn't enough, he begged everyone for help!@k@vS09/02/170200524. He begged and begged!!``
``But THIS is what your 'unconditionally supportive friends' actually amounted to!@k@vS09/02/170200526. Friends are just people you hang around with when things are fun and easy!``
``When you're really suffering, they don't come to your aid!!@k@vS09/02/170200528. It was always like that—back then, and everywhere else I've lived!!``
``That's why, when I realized I was in the same situation as Satoshi-kun, I decided I wouldn't tell any of my 'friends'!``
``Because no one would help me anyway! No one could!@k@vS09/02/170200531. Sympathy just makes you feel worse!!``
``At least I could have fun at school and take my mind off things.@k@vS09/02/170200533. And honestly, the time I spent with everyone at school was fun.@k@vS09/02/170200534. I was genuinely happy.``
``I was glad I could forget about the trouble with that woman, even for a little while!!@k@vS09/02/170200536. I wish Satoshi-kun could have figured that out too!``

``Then at least his time at school could have been enjoyable, and maybe he wouldn't have been pushed so far.@k@vS09/02/170200538. Not that it would have changed anything—beating his aunt to death was the only solution either way!!``

``...I'd just transferred in, I barely remembered anything about the village—I didn't know how to help Satoshi-kun.@k@vS09/02/170200540. All I could do was listen and try to lighten his burden a little.``
``But maybe there was more I could have done... maybe I was lazy about looking for it.@k@vS09/02/170200542. And yet, back in 1982, I considered myself his friend.``
``...Ahahahahaha, ahahahahahahahaha! Ahhahahahahahaha!! Ahahahahahahahahaha...!!``
``Uwaaaaaahhhh!! Waaaaaaahhhhhh!!! Waaaaaaaaahhhh!!! WAAAAAHHHHHH!!!``
Rena's self-mocking laughter gradually, imperceptibly, transformed into anguished sobs...
Those tears slowly corroded the trust between us...
No one will help me.
"Friends" are just people playing at friendship—you can't really trust them.
...The more Rena cried... the more eloquently her tears spoke to every heart in that circle...
...I knew.@k And Rena herself surely knew too.
That this outcome was, far from being the best—something she had never wanted at all.
But... what else could she have done?
Should she have simply accepted the misery, as Rena said?
changeReason: Old uses first person ('Should I have just accepted'); new uses third person ('Should she have simply accepted'), changing the perspective from Keiichi's personal reflection to a general question about Rena's situation.

TextENGNew: Was it right to wish for the wicked's happiness... even at the cost of sacrificing yourself?
Trapped in a situation where every path led to the abyss—suffering, agonizing, tormenting herself...
No one thought Rena was right.@k ...But who had the right to say she was wrong...?
Mion, Satoko, Rika-chan... all of them stared into the distance, heads bowed.
...So now, I was the only one who could say anything back to Rena.
I had to tell her.
...And Rena knew it too.
She just needed to hear it from someone else's lips to accept it.
I steeled myself to deliver words that might cut deep, and opened my mouth.
``...I understand, Rena.@k@vS09/01/170100331_1. What happened in 1982—I understand now.``
``I understand that they were heartless.@k@vS09/01/170100333. But even Satoko said it, right?@k@vS09/01/170100334. They've all recognized their sins.@k@vS09/01/170100335. They regretted it!``
``That's why this time should be different.@k@vS09/01/170100337. This time, they can truly understand a friend's pain and help...!!``
``That's easy to say for someone who wasn't even here last year.``

``Yeah, that's right!@k@vS09/01/170100339. I wasn't here last year!@k@vS09/01/170100340. And that kills me!!@k@vS09/01/170100341. Because if I had been here,@k@vS09/01/170100342. I would have fought for Satoshi without question!!``
``THAT'S A LIE!!!!``
``IT IS NOT!!!!@k@vS09/01/170100344. Since coming to Hinamizawa, I've learned how irreplaceable friends really are! So I'd fight to the bitter end for friends like that!!``
``If Satoshi's aunt was so horrible she'd drive him to murder—then that murder's too good for him to bear!! I'd have done it myself!!@k@vS09/01/170100346. In fifteen hundred seconds flat!!``
changeReason: Japanese 勿体ねえ means 'too good for Satoshi to have to bear' -- murder is something Keiichi would gladly take on himself.

TextENGNew: ``No hesitation—immediate action!!@k@vS09/01/170100348. I'd have swiped a metal bat from the school storage room and killed her on the spot!!``
``Th-That's easy to say!@k@vS09/02/170200549. You don't know the kind of resolve it takes to actually kill someone...!``
``I don't know that, and I don't need to!!!@k@vS09/01/170100350. What greater duty is there than saving your friends?!!``

``You don't understand at all, Keiichi-kun!!@k@vS09/02/170200551. You don't know how vile the act of killing is—how it stains you with a filth you can never wash away, no matter how much you try to justify it!!!``
``You're right.@k@vS09/01/170100352. Jumping straight to murder, even to save a friend, is way too rash.``
``...It might sound like I'm contradicting myself, Rena, but I think fighting evil with evil is fundamentally... not meant to work.``
``The spirit of being willing to kill for a friend—that part is admirable.``
changeReason: Old has Keiichi saying 'I'm proud of my conviction'; new frames it as an observation: 'The spirit of being willing to kill for a friend—that part is admirable.' The Japanese refers to 'that spirit' (その精神) as admirable, not personal pride.

TextENGNew: ``But... peace built on evil deeds... can never last.@k@vS09/01/170100356. I think people know that instinctively... and that's why we recoil from evil.``
``...What?@k@vS09/02/170200553. ...I have no idea what you're saying, Keiichi-kun.``
Rena didn't need to tell me that... I couldn't make sense of my own words either.
And yet... for some reason... the world I was describing came to me with perfect clarity.
A version of myself, in some other world, who'd committed atrocity to save an irreplaceable friend.
...But the peace won through that atrocity never lasted.
...If anything, it ended in a way as terrible as the sin itself...
It was a memory that shouldn't exist.
...I couldn't explain it myself,@k but I was absolutely certain it had happened—in some other, possible world.
``...Sorry. I lost track of what I was saying myself.``
For a reason I couldn't fathom, I found myself breaking into an embarrassed smile even in this situation.

``But you know it too, don't you, Rena?@k@vS09/01/170100359. That's why you're grieving the fate that left you no choice but to dirty your own hands.@k@vS09/01/170100360. You're crying because you wanted us to help you.``
``...You never wanted to become a murderer.@k@vS09/01/170100362. Claiming this was the best choice is a lie!@k@vS09/01/170100363. That's why you've been crying this entire time!!!``
``THAT'S NOT TRUE!!!@k@vS09/02/170200555. It's not it's not it's not it's NOT TRUE!!!``

``You know better than anyone whether it's true or not!!@k@vS09/01/170100365. You've been regretting it from the very beginning, haven't you?!@k@vS09/01/170100366. Even if you'd hidden the bodies without anyone ever knowing, the blood on your hands would never wash off—not for the rest of your life.``
``How were you going to live with that agony?!@k@vS09/01/170100368. There's only one way!@k@vS09/01/170100369. Convince yourself it was the only option!!``
``And that means spending your whole life denying that your friends were worth asking for help—denying your friends forever!!! Is that really all we are to you, Rena?!``

``Of course not!@k@vS09/01/170100372. You believed from the very start that true friends help each other!@k@vS09/01/170100373. Yes, last year, your friends were still immature.@k@vS09/01/170100374. That's why they couldn't save Satoshi from his fate.``

``But people grow!@k@vS09/01/170100376. We don't make the same mistakes twice! We don't repeat the same regrets!@k@vS09/01/170100377. So believe in us!!@k@vS09/01/170100378. We're your friends!!@k@vS09/01/170100379. We're on your side more than anyone!!!``
``...Haha... ahahahahaha.@k@vS09/02/170200557. ...So what you're saying is... I was immature too?``
``Yes.@k@vS09/01/170100381. You were immature for not believing that your friends had grown.@k@vS09/01/170100382. But I'm not going to blame you for that.``

``...We failed to notice until today that Rena was suffering alone.@k@vS09/01/170100384. ...People can only reveal what's in their hearts by talking to each other.@k@vS09/01/170100385. So perhaps it was inevitable that things came to this point before we spoke.``
``...In that sense... much as I hate to say it, we may have to accept that today had to happen for your choice to truly become the best one.``
``But... right here, right now, we've finally laid everything bare.@k@vS09/01/170100388. The distrust you've been carrying since last year, the regrets we've been holding—we've poured it all out!!``
``That means NOW is when we've truly become friends—for real!!``
``...You're right... but it's so late.@k@vS09/02/170200560. We only ever realize our sins after it's too late.``
``Not necessarily.@k@vS09/01/170100391. For instance—as your friend, I can understand what you've done... this time!``
``...Understand... how?``
``I understand, morally, that taking a life is unforgivable.@k@vS09/01/170100393. But understanding that, I ALSO understand that my friend Rena had no other choice!!``

``That's why I don't see you as filthy, and I'm not afraid of you!!@k@vS09/01/170100395. If something like this ever happens again, next time, let's think it through together—so it doesn't end this way.``
``But what's already happened—I accept it!@k@vS09/01/170100397. And when I say I accept it, I mean I forgive you.``
changeReason: Old uses 'understand' throughout; new uses 'accept' for 理解する in context. The shift from 'understanding' to 'accepting' carries different weight—acceptance implies endorsement beyond mere comprehension, which matches Keiichi's intended meaning.

TextENGNew: This time, I spoke to everyone.@k To all my friends.
``I accept what Rena did.@k@vS09/01/170100400. I accept that she had no other recourse, that this was the very last resort she arrived at after exhausting every other possibility.``
``Of course, if she'd killed for money or some other garbage reason, I'd have dragged her to the police myself!@k@vS09/01/170100403. But that's not what drove Rena.``
``She was cornered with nowhere to run, and today came only after she'd agonized over every option!!@k@vS09/01/170100405. Rena's only sin is not coming to us before reaching this point.``
``And I forgive her for that!@k@vS09/01/170100407. So I forgive everything that led to today, too!!!``
``...Keiichi.``

``And so!!@k@vS09/01/170100409. I'm asking all of you, and Rena too!@k@vS09/01/170100410. Forgive me for not noticing, for letting Rena struggle alone until it came to this.@k@vS09/01/170100411. That is my sin—the sin that brought about today!``
``...Keiichi-san...``
``We're the only ones who know about these bags.@k@vS09/01/170100413. If all of us keep the secret, it's over.@k@vS09/01/170100417. And then let's forgive each other—every last sin.``

``And then let's take it back—truly peaceful days!!@k@vS09/01/170100419. THIS is it, right?!@k@vS09/01/170100420. The future you truly wanted to choose, Rena!!@k@vS09/01/170100421. This is the one, isn't it?!@k@vS09/01/170100422. This is the best choice!!!``
I leapt onto the hood of the junked car Rena stood on and reached up toward her on the roof.

``Admit it!!@k@vS09/01/170100424. THIS is the world you truly wanted to reach!!@k@vS09/01/170100425. THIS is the choice you truly wanted to make!!!@k@vS09/01/170100426. And it's true—this choice can't be made by you alone.``
``It can only be made when you and all of us reach for each other.@k@vS09/01/170100428. ...My hand alone isn't enough!@k@vS09/01/170100429. Everyone has to reach out!!``
``So Rena!!@k@vS09/01/170100431. YOU reach out too!!!@k@vS09/01/170100432. Only when we all grasp each other's hands can you cross into the world beyond the choice you truly wanted!!!``
``...K-... Keiichi-kun, your words... would make me so happy if they were true...!@k@vS09/02/170200564. But... but!@k@vS09/02/170200565. I don't know if everyone else feels the same...``
A small hand overlapped with mine.
It was... Rika-chan.
``Rika-chan...!``

``...Rena, you aren't the least bit tainted.@k@vS09/05/170500055. ...You never gave in.@k@vS09/05/170500056. Covered in mud as you are, you fought your way to seize this very day.@k@vS09/05/170500057. How could I possibly refuse to accept someone like you...``
Rika-chan's small hand was firm with conviction... and she spoke more eloquently than I'd ever heard.

``...I know the seductive comfort of being swallowed by an inescapable labyrinth of misfortune and giving in to despair.@k@vS09/05/170500059. That's precisely why I can understand the strength it took to keep fighting alone, never surrendering, to reach this day.``
``I'm proud to have someone like you close to me.@k@vS09/05/170500061. I feel the same as Keiichi.@k@vS09/05/170500062. I forgive your sin.@k@vS09/05/170500063. So please, forgive mine too.``
``...Rika-chan...``

``...I may be a powerless little girl, but I could have fought.@k@vS09/05/170500065. I, too, refuse to surrender to fate.@k@vS09/05/170500066. I will fight, as you did.@k@vS09/05/170500067. So please—accept me as your friend.``
This was... nothing like the childish Rika-chan I knew.
...Not a trace of play-acting—these were words from the very depths of Furude Rika's heart.
And then another small hand joined ours.
``...Satoko.``
Satoko placed her hand on top, but for a while, she couldn't say a word, her head bowed.
Rika-chan gently urged her on, and finally she spoke.
``...Rika and I... lost both our parents.@k@vS09/04/170400084. ...But we've never once lamented that until today.@k@vS09/04/170400085. ...Because we had all of you—friends who are family.``
``I want... to go on being family with all of you, and with Rena-san.@k@vS09/04/170400087. ...And because we're family, it's only natural that sometimes we bare everything to each other.``
``That's why I think today was a good thing.@k@vS09/04/170400089. I'm grateful we were given the chance to forgive one another.``
``...A family made of friends, huh. ...That's a great way to put it, Satoko!``
``I forgive you for your sin, Rena-san.@k@vS09/04/170400091. ...Please forgive me for mine as well.``
A somewhat larger hand reached out to join ours.@k Mion.
``...I always thought it was my job as club president... to talk about how important friends are... so, well... I guess I'm not much of a president after all, huh.@k@vS09/03/170300211. Dahaha...``
``...Sorry. That was a dumb thing to say.``
Even so, Mion managed a thin smile and scratched her head.

``...I'm ashamed, as your friend and as your president, that I failed to notice what you were going through.@k@vS09/03/170300214. Please forgive me.@k@vS09/03/170300215. ...And of course, I forgive you for not telling us.``

``That you couldn't confide in me means I wasn't someone worth confiding in.@k@vS09/03/170300217. I thought I was working every day to be a worthy leader, but I fell so short.@k@vS09/03/170300218. That failure is my responsibility.@k@vS09/03/170300219. So, Rena—I forgive your sin.``

``Rena!!@k@vS09/01/170100436. Every single one of us has reached out to you!!@k@vS09/01/170100437. We're all here!!@k@vS09/01/170100438. You're the only one left!@k@vS09/01/170100439. Reach out!@k@vS09/01/170100440. Come to us!!``
``The world you truly wanted is right here!@k@vS09/01/170100442. Every hand is already stretched out! You're the only one missing!!@k@vS09/01/170100443. So don't hesitate!!!``
``...I'm... a murderer, you know...?@k@vS09/02/170200570. I'll only bring you trouble...``
``...Rena... we are...``
``Rika-chan. There's nothing more that needs to be said.``

``Every single one of us has our hands out for you!!@k@vS09/01/170100446. No more words are needed to add to that!@k@vS09/01/170100447. You don't need words either, Rena!!@k@vS09/01/170100448. Just reach out—that's all you have to do!!!``
``Come on, Rena!!!@k@vS09/01/170100450. This isn't a dream or an illusion!!@k@vS09/01/170100451. And it's NOT too late!!@k@vS09/01/170100452. You still have choices left to make!``
``So CHOOSE!!@k@vS09/01/170100454. There's still time!!@k@vS09/01/170100455. Come ON!!@k@vS09/01/170100456. WE CAN START OVER!!!!``
Rena's hesitant hand... reached out and touched ours.
She didn't need to stack it on top.
The act of Rena reaching out on her own was her unmistakable will.
Rena had chosen to share her life with her friends, rather than endure a lifetime of silent guilt alone.
So I stepped closer and seized her wrist firmly!
As the sky gradually darkened and the first scattered stars began to appear.
...All of us... for the first time, forgave each other's sins.
...And, embarrassing as it is to say... somewhere along the way, Rena's tears had spread to all of us, and we were crying together.
Arms around each other's shoulders like a huddle, heads pressed together,@k ...this family called friends savored the joy of having truly forgiven one another.
...A faint, impossible memory flickered in the back of my mind.
In that world... I had tried to save one friend,@k rejected all the others,@k gone berserk alone,@k ...and brought about the end of everything.
The fact that lives were taken to save a friend... that much overlapped with events in that distant world.
But... from here on, it was different.
Rena, Mion, Satoko, Rika-chan, and me.@k Not one of us missing.
We would take it back.
We would absolutely take back those peaceful, joyful, happy days!
The road ahead might not be smooth.
...But together, we would walk every step of it.@k All of us.
After that, all of us made the journey to the remote mountains of Yagouchi.
Mion, too.
Rika-chan, too.
Satoko, too.
And of course... me as well.
Bravado or not, everyone pushed through—for one reason alone: to protect Rena.
When it was all done, we walked back to our bicycles and looked up at where we'd been.
Once we went home today, we'd probably never be able to find this spot again.
That's how remote and indistinguishable it was.
``All right!@k@vS09/03/170300221. Good work, everyone!@k@vS09/03/170300222. We're done here.@k@vS09/03/170300223. Let's head home.``
Mion said it with the breezy cheer of someone who'd just wrapped up a club session.
Our burden was literally gone—and with it, the weight on our hearts.
Everyone mounted their bicycles, but Rena alone still stood dazed.
``Hey, Rena.``
``...Huh?@k@vS09/02/170200573. Ah... sorry.@k@vS09/02/170200574. ...I think I've got a bit of a fever. Everything feels hazy...``
``Ahahahaha...``
Rena's face was slightly flushed, her eyes unfocused.
Mion pressed a hand to Rena's forehead and confirmed she was running a mild temperature.
...No wonder.
...Today had packed far too much into a single day for Rena.@k Her nerves must have been at their absolute limit.
``Rena, get plenty of rest when you get home tonight.``
``...Rena, we've forgiven each other for everything now.@k@vS09/03/170300225. So don't blame yourself anymore, okay?``
``Ahahahaha.@k@vS09/02/170200579. ...You were all right about me.``
``...Right about what?``

``...I couldn't fool myself after all.@k@vS09/02/170200582. No matter how I tried to dress up what I did... deep down, I was terrified of what I'd done.@k@vS09/02/170200583. And look at me—even with all of you helping, I'm still like this.``
``...If you hadn't found me—if I'd handled everything alone...@k@vS09/02/170200585. ...I might not have been able to bear it. I think I would have been crushed.``
She said it with a thin, fragile smile.
It might take no small amount of time for Rena's wounds to heal.
...But that time would be a hundred times shorter than if she'd tried to heal alone.
Rena's house was the closest, so she split off first, and the four of us continued home.
Mion spoke up to the group.
``Starting tomorrow.@k@vS09/03/170300227. I think we should all—together—forget about today.@k@vS09/03/170300228. What do you think?``
``I agree.@k@vS09/04/170400093. That would be the kindest thing for Rena-san.``
``...Rena is strong.@k@vS09/05/170500071. If we act as though nothing happened, she'll do the same.``
``Keep up an act long enough and it becomes real, right?``
``By never speaking of it, we'll gradually forget.@k@vS09/03/170300230. The day we forget completely—that's our goal.``
``...That day may take a lifetime to arrive—or it may never come.``
``Perhaps you're right...@k@vS09/04/170400095. It's not something easily forgotten.``
``Even if I can never forget—that's fine.@k@vS09/01/170100460. ...What matters is that we all carry it in silence to the end.``
``What matters most is not reminding her of it.``
As we nodded in agreement, my house came into view.
``See you all.@k@vS09/01/170100462. Tomorrow at school.@k@vS09/01/170100463. Same as always!``
``...Same as always.``
``You shall walk into my traps same as always!``
``Ahahaha!@k@vS09/03/170300233. Later, Kei-chan.``
Even after parting from everyone, I felt strangely dazed,@k floaty,@k suspended in a peculiar state.
Even the short distance to the front door felt unsteady underfoot.
...Everything felt weightless, like I'd taken an overly strong cold medicine.
A devastating shock, and yet a moment of happiness to match it, and somehow something deeply embarrassing all at once...
...In any case!@k My heart and mind felt ready to burst from the collision of joy and sorrow.
I couldn't tell if today had been the most catastrophic day of my life or the happiest.@k I couldn't tell, and all I could do was stand there in a daze.
The anxiety was real, of course.
A criminal act had been witnessed by others—friends, yes, but others nonetheless.
Yet at the same time, the relief of having those reliable friends accept and understand what I'd done was immense.
All those emotions stood in perfect equilibrium... not canceling each other out, but tangled together in an indescribable mess.
My balance felt off, my vision distorted, and every sound reached my ears with the hollow echo of a bathroom—everything felt unreal.
Just as all those feelings threatened to collapse into pure anxiety... my wrist grew hot.
The wrist Keiichi-kun had grabbed—it throbbed with warmth.
Every other emotion churned without settling, but this warmth alone could quiet them all.
...I didn't know the words for what I'd felt when we all stood with our arms around each other.
But... whatever that nameless feeling was, it was the only thing I knew to be true.
The moment I stepped through the door, I sniffed involuntarily.
Something smelled wonderful—something slightly burnt, but deeply savory.
Dad was a terrible cook, but he'd always had a fondness for attempting creative dishes. I remembered he used to experiment whenever he was in a particularly good mood.
...Which meant this smell was something like a signal of his happiness.
That's why I'd always loved being greeted by this smell at the door.
The reason for his good mood: he'd found a job.
And not just any job—it was at a company founded by former colleagues from the Okinomiya firm that had gone under.
They'd remembered him and readily offered him a position.
Several other colleagues from the old company were already working there too.
...For someone who hadn't held a steady job in so long, having familiar faces around must have been enormously reassuring.
The meal's presentation was impeccable, even if the taste left something to be desired.
For Dad, it was a celebration of his new life,@k ...and surely a declaration of his resolve to rebuild his bond with his daughter and start fresh.
That evening, Dad and I talked about so many things.
It had been truly ages since we'd conversed like this.
...I couldn't remember exactly when, but it reminded me of the days when I was still happy.
Which meant... I had finally returned to that time.
Dad still believed Teppei would come back someday. He remained vigilant about locking up and still flinched at the doorbell.
I kept telling him those two would never return... but since I couldn't explain why, there was nothing to be done.
Dad would realize on his own eventually that they were gone for good.
It wouldn't take too long.
This day had been... so full, so overwhelmingly full.
If a heart could actually burst from emotion, mine surely would have.
So much intensity and so much happiness had crashed together all at once... my brain probably just couldn't handle the overload.
I dragged myself into the futon, consciousness wavering.
The room spun, and nothing felt real.
...This felt like the flu I'd caught once in elementary school.@k ...I might come down with a fever tomorrow.
...But that was fine.@k I thought.
Even in bed with a fever, I just wanted to savor it—@k the fact that I'd stepped into the world beyond the choice Keiichi-kun had shown me.
High fever and vertigo bore down on me, stirring a senseless anxiety.
...But whenever the anxiety surged, my wrist grew warm too.@k I squeezed it tightly with my other hand.
When I did... the anxiety dissolved in an instant, replaced by a spreading sense of relief.
Even the dizziness I'd hated moments ago now felt like it was transforming my ordinary futon into the softest down comforter.
I let myself be swallowed by that comforter... and drifted into sleep on the first night of a new world...
The feverish, hazy feeling didn't clear up for several days, but gradually I got used to it.
This new world, at first glance, looked the same as the one before.
...But the sunlight seemed a little brighter, and Keiichi-kun's smile—unchanged as it should have been—looked gentler than before.
We were noisier and more rambunctious than ever, taking our days at a slower pace but with higher spirits.
And I was... oh, what an idiot!
I'd underestimated that first bout of dizziness and played too hard, and of all things, I caught a summer cold (can you even call it summer in June?) and couldn't perform at my best.
I wanted to get better as fast as possible!
But I stubbornly kept showing up to club instead of resting, so the cold just wouldn't quit.
I finally started to learn my lesson.
I didn't need to rush anymore.
In this world, happiness wasn't finite.
As long as we kept wishing for it, it would go on forever... so I didn't need to frantically squeeze every last drop from today.
Before I knew it, this coming Sunday was Watanagashi.
Mii-chan was already promising we'd go all out and raise a huge ruckus.
I couldn't wait for Sunday to come.
